highlights.title: Star Google Researcher Joins OpenAI in Coup for ChatGPT Creator
highlights.description: One of Google’s most prominent researchers is leaving for rival OpenAI, dealing a setback to Alphabet Inc. in a multibillion-dollar race to build the world’s most powerful artificial intelligence models.

highlights.title: Google Gemini co-lead Noam Shazeer leaves for OpenAI
highlights.description: Google's vice president of engineering and a co-leader of its Gemini AI models announced Wednesday he is leaving the company to join OpenAI.

highlights.title: Gemini’s co-lead is leaving Google to join OpenAI
highlights.description: VP of engineering at Google and the co-lead of Gemini, Noam Shazeer, has announced he is departing his role to work at OpenAI.

highlights.title: Anthropic Ban Forces Investor Rethink of Political Risk
highlights.description: Investors in the ever-hotter AI stock rally must suddenly consider a risk with the potential to be even more damaging than high valuations and big spending: Politics getting in the way.

highlights.title: Midjourney goes from generating cat images to full-body ultrasound scans
highlights.description:
“A scan of an imaging phantom, segmented to validate how cleanly structures separate under controlled conditions.“ | Image: Midjourney Medical	

Midjourney CEO David Holz just showed off the company's first hardware product and plans to build a San Francisco spa, which he admitted is a bit different from the "cat pictures" produced by its AI image generator. Dubbed The Midjourney Scanner, it's an ultrasound-based full-body scanner that uses a ring of sensors to capture vertical slices of the inside of your body, looking at the composition of your muscle, fat, bone, and organs to start. Holz said ideally, you could do this once a year or every single day, as it "aims for image quality comparable to MRI in many ways."
He mentioned that one way he'd like to use it would be to see how h …
Read the full story at The Verge.

highlights.title: Intel surges 9% after Trump says company will partner with Apple on U.S. chip design
highlights.description: The boost continues Intel's recent rally as the chipmaker bounces back from years of headwinds.

highlights.title: Plurimi CIO Sees Value in AI Supply Chain
highlights.description: Plurimi Wealth Chief Investment Officer Patrick Armstrong says he sees value in the artificial intelligence supply chain, stocks such as Micron, Samsung and Nvidia. "We're underweight the Magnificent Seven — the hyperscalers — those are the companies that are spending a trillion dollars over the next 12 months on AI datacenters," Armstrong tells Bloomberg Television. "And we own the companies they're giving the money to." (Source: Bloomberg)

highlights.title: SpaceX Shares Waver After Posting First Decline Since IPO
highlights.description: SpaceX shares fluctuated in premarket trading following the previous session’s drop, as it wraps up its first week as a public company following a record-breaking listing.

highlights.title: Apple and Intel are working together to build chips in America, says President Trump
highlights.description:
President Trump took to TruthSocial tonight to confirm what was previously rumored: Apple and Intel have agreed a partnership where Intel will manufacture chips for future Apple devices, using Intel’s American-based fabrication sites.
The breadth and depth of the agreement is unclear. It is unlikely that Intel will be able to supply the latest-generation chips for Apple’s highest-end iPhones, any time soon.

highlights.title: Apple to Make Chips in US With Intel, Trump Says
highlights.description:
Apple has agreed to work with Intel to manufacture some of its chips in the United States, U.S. president Trump said on Thursday.


Intel's stock rose 9 percent in premarket trading following Trump's comments, which appeared in a social media post. Apple was up 0.6 percent in premarket trading.

The Wall Street Journal reported last month that Apple and Intel had established a preliminary agreement that would see Intel manufacturing processors for Apple devices.

Intel would make chips based on Apple chip designs in the same way that TSMC currently does. Prior rumors on Intel's Apple talks have suggested Intel could make some of the lower-end processors used in Apple devices, including the lowest-end M-series chip used in select iPad and Mac models.

Before Apple adopted Apple silicon, it used Intel-designed chips for its Macs, but there were continual chip delays. Today, Apple designs its own Arm-based chips and has TSMC manufacture them, giving it the freedom to roll out updates on a more predictable schedule.

Apple hasn't looked to Intel as a supplier before, both because the chipmaker has trailed rivals like TSMC and Samsung and because of the rocky history between the two companies. 

That picture may be shifting under Lip-Bu Tan, who took over as Intel CEO last year after the company ousted Pat Gelsinger, and has since pushed to turn around its struggling chip manufacturing arm. Tan's efforts appear to be having the desired impact – the company's stock has surged 464 percent in the past 12 months, with the company hitting a market cap of $608.7 billion. The U.S. government also took a 10 percent stake in Intel last year, converting $8.9 billion in unpaid Chips Act grants into equity.

constrained during the quarter because the company couldn't secure enough A19 and A19 Pro chips from TSMC.

Tag: Intel
This article, "Apple to Make Chips in US With Intel, Trump Says" first appeared on MacRumors.com
Discuss this article in our forums

ai.title: Oracle support timelines for Fusion Middleware tighter than expected
ai.description: Big Red drops ominous mention of 'Market Driven Support' beyond 2027 - but there's good news for AIX users

ai.title: AI Startup Midjourney Pivots to Health With Ultrasound Machine
ai.description: AI startup Midjourney Inc. announced its first hardware project at an event in San Francisco, outlining an unexpected move into the personal health and medical industries.

ai.title: Midjourney, the AI image generator, is developing a full-body ultrasonic scanner
ai.description: Midjourney has announced its first hardware project, and it can do more than make cute AI pet photos.

ai.title: AI for Industry Is Europe’s Hope to Salvage Manufacturing Edge
ai.description: Pressure to become more efficient has Europe racing to bring artificial intelligence to the shop floor.

ai.title: China AI Lab’s 170% Stock Surge Cements Winner-Loser Pair Trade
ai.description: A pair trade is emerging in China’s artificial intelligence sector, with investors piling into the perceived winner and betting against its rival seen as losing ground in the race to build commercially viable AI models.

ai.title: Blackstone’s AirTrunk Seeks $3 Billion Loan for Sydney Project
ai.description: AirTrunk Pty., a data center operator backed by Blackstone Inc., is in talks with banks for a A$4.3 billion ($3 billion) loan to back a new project in Australia, people familiar with the matter said, as it continues its debt-fueled expansion across Asia Pacific.

ai.title: Gemini for Android using Bubbles to let you multitask & continue chats
ai.description: After adding Dynamic Color earlier this month, the Gemini overlay on Android is now using Bubbles to help keep track of conversations.

ai.title: Digital entrepreneur creates humorous 'physical NFT minting device' using a Raspberry Pi in quest for 'infinite money machine' — contraption trained on M3 MacBook can generate an NFT in 3 seconds, has so far sold one for $9.92
ai.description: An enterprising young man aims to catch up with the collective wealth of Elon Musk, and his first money spinner is a portable NFT minting gadget.

ai.title: CME chief executive says company plans to sue CFTC after perpetual futures approval
ai.description: Kalshi's perpetual futures product did not meet the Dodd-Frank Act's definition of a "swap" and should not have been approved, Terrence Duffy said.

ev_autonomous.title: SpaceX Shares Waver After Posting First Decline Since IPO
ev_autonomous.description: SpaceX shares fluctuated in premarket trading following the previous session’s drop, as it wraps up its first week as a public company following a record-breaking listing.

space.title: NASA picks Eric Schmidt’s rocket company for Mars mission, setting up a race with SpaceX
space.description: Relativity Space—a rocket maker acquired by former Google executive chair Eric Schmidt last year after stumbling on the path to orbit—might just beat SpaceX to Mars.

space.title: SpaceX shares hold steady after three-day rally loses steam
space.description: Investor bullishness showed signs of waning on Wednesday, as shares sank 5%.

business.title: AI Company Dream Triples Value to $3 Billion in Funding Round
business.description: Dream, an Israeli artificial intelligence company that provides AI and cybersecurity services to governments and critical infrastructure operators, has raised $260 million at a $3 billion valuation.

regulation.title: Korea Rejects Baemin, Coupang Settlement Bids in Antitrust Probe
regulation.description: South Korea’s antitrust watchdog has rejected settlement proposals from the country’s two largest food delivery platforms, Baedal Minjok and Coupang Eats, leaving both firms exposed to potentially significant fines over alleged unfair business practices.
